title: Daily Briefing — May 21
headline: Rotation begins, bonds wobble, and tech still rules the flows—patience required.
body: Right, so the big picture: Market's hanging at ATHs with SPY ($738.14) well above the 20 MA ($729.77), VIX in a normal regime (17.72), and Fear & Greed dialed back to 60.8—so not frothy, but still leaning greed. We're not in 'chase everything' mode, but flows remain bullish, especially in mega-cap tech.

Sector-wise, Energy (XLE $60.17) and Tech (XLK $177.13) are solidly leading, both above key MAs. XLB, XLI, XLC are lagging and risk-rated high. Rotation is starting to show—watch for defensive sectors like Utilities (XLU $44.56, elevated risk but improving), Staples (XLP $83.79), and Real Estate (XLRE $44.05) picking up momentum but only as a potential hiding spot if macro cracks. Financials (XLF $51.29) are approaching support, so that's your canary—don't ignore.

Contrarian radar: HYG ($79.68) and TLT ($83.49) both triggered bearish signals—if bonds freak out, everything breaks (R001). Tech still gets flow love, but late-cycle defensive has started to rotate. No extreme sentiment readings, but keep an eye on the AAII Bulls, Smart Money, and dark pools—flows first, opinions last. Don't chase moves; abundance mindset means you wait for the next A+ setup.
callout_body:
SPY: $738.14 (watch for daily close below 20 MA $729.77)
XLK: $177.13 (firmly above 20 MA $169.48)
XLF: $51.29 (approaching 200 MA $52.27—canary alert)
XLE: $60.17 (rotation leader, above 20 MA $58.40)
TLT: $83.49 (triggered bond risk)
Fear & Greed: 60.8 (no extreme yet)
Don't chase ARM ($281.88, +9.8%) or NBIS ($211.92, +10.5%)—wait for retests.
action_items: Reduce equity exposure if HYG/TLT continue breaking down—macro rules first.
Stay overweight Tech (AAPL $301.91, NVDA $224.00, DELL $245.29) while above 20 MA, but rotate toward Energy (XLE) as flows shift.
closing_quote: Patience, react, don't predict—if it's in the press, it's already in the price.
